  CL9NK Entry: perfect_cuboid
  An Euler brick where additionally the space diagonal sqrt(a¬≤+b¬≤+c¬≤) is integer. Equivalent to finding a perfect
  rectangular cuboid with all 7 distances (3 edges, 3 face diagonals, 1 space diagonal) being integers. Unknown
  existence ‚Äî oldest open problem in Diophantine geometry, equivalent to a simultaneous system of 4 Pythagorean
  equations.

imasm <op> ‚Ä¶ ‚Äî compose the 12 opcodes into a free polymer TOPOLOGY.
Ops: `chain T1 T2‚Ä¶` a strand ¬∑ `ring T1 T2‚Ä¶` a cycle (fork/fuse NOT reconnected) ¬∑ `protocol T1 T2‚Ä¶` an opcode word built so its ‚óá/‚óè pairs RECONNECT (Œ¥ arm ‚Üí Œº) ‚Äî this is how you CLOSE a protocol from a sequence; a naive `ring` leaves the fork dangling and Œº‚àòŒ¥ OPEN, and a protocol does NOT close by looping back to ‚ä¢ (a source) ¬∑ `star CORE : arm1 : arm2 : arm3` a hub with f‚â•3 arms (K(1,f), œÅ=‚àöf) ¬∑ `comb BACKBONE : P arm : Q arm` a backbone with pendant grafts ¬∑ `bubble PRE : A : B : POST` a ‚óá‚Üí(A|B)‚Üí‚óè fork that reconverges ¬∑ `wire N0 N1 ‚Ä¶ / i-j i-k ‚Ä¶` FREE composition of ANY graph from an explicit node and directed edge set (Œ≤>1, fused rings, cross-branch, non-planar ‚Äî the primitive the others specialize) ¬∑ `classify T1 T2‚Ä¶` read a flat line and name it ¬∑ `ref` the rules.
Each build reports the topology label, circuit rank Œ≤=E‚àíV+C, branch/merge/source/sink census, arm count, œÅ, and grammar validity. An arm that runs out is a living end, not an error. Distinct from the monomer verbs (forge/polymerize), which fuse named catalog entries.
STRANGE LOOP: the 49 Shavian TYPES the Grammar writes tuples with are themselves full IMASM programs ‚Äî `imasm types` lists them, `imasm expand <type>` unfolds one into its own opcode sequence. Splice an expanded type's sequence into a polymer arm to pivot through state space AS that type; the alphabet's letters are words in the same language, so composition recurses.

imasm <op> ‚Ä¶ ‚Äî compose the 12 opcodes into a free polymer TOPOLOGY.
Ops: `chain T1 T2‚Ä¶` a strand ¬∑ `ring T1 T2‚Ä¶` a cycle (fork/fuse NOT reconnected) ¬∑ `protocol T1 T2‚Ä¶` an opcode word built so its ‚óá/‚óè pairs RECONNECT (Œ¥ arm ‚Üí Œº) ‚Äî this is how you CLOSE a protocol from a sequence; a naive `ring` leaves the fork dangling and Œº‚àòŒ¥ OPEN, and a protocol does NOT close by looping back to ‚ä¢ (a source) ¬∑ `star CORE : arm1 : arm2 : arm3` a hub with f‚â•3 arms (K(1,f), œÅ=‚àöf) ¬∑ `comb BACKBONE : P arm : Q arm` a backbone with pendant grafts ¬∑ `bubble PRE : A : B : POST` a ‚óá‚Üí(A|B)‚Üí‚óè fork that reconverges ¬∑ `wire N0 N1 ‚Ä¶ / i-j i-k ‚Ä¶` FREE composition of ANY graph from an explicit node and directed edge set (Œ≤>1, fused rings, cross-branch, non-planar ‚Äî the primitive the others specialize) ¬∑ `classify T1 T2‚Ä¶` read a flat line and name it ¬∑ `ref` the rules.
Each build reports the topology label, circuit rank Œ≤=E‚àíV+C, branch/merge/source/sink census, arm count, œÅ, and grammar validity. An arm that runs out is a living end, not an error. Distinct from the monomer verbs (forge/polymerize), which fuse named catalog entries.
STRANGE LOOP: the 49 Shavian TYPES the Grammar writes tuples with are themselves full IMASM programs ‚Äî `imasm types` lists them, `imasm expand <type>` unfolds one into its own opcode sequence. Splice an expanded type's sequence into a polymer arm to pivot through state space AS that type; the alphabet's letters are words in the same language, so composition recurses.
